sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Edward Jenner
translatedText: Edward Jenner

sourceText: In the late 18th century, smallpox was a terrifying scourge, claiming countless lives globally. With no effective cure, people lived in fear, and the toll on children was particularly devastating. As a physician, witnessing such suffering was a profound anguish.
translatedText: Mwishoni mwa karne ya 18, ugonjwa wa ndui ulikuwa janga la kutisha, likigharimu maisha mengi ulimwenguni. Kwa kukosa tiba inayofaa, watu waliishi kwa hofu, na matokeo ya watoto yalikuwa yenye kuumiza sana. Akiwa daktari, kushuhudia mateso hayo kulikuwa uchungu mwingi.

sourceText: I observed that milkmaids who contracted cowpox seemed immune to smallpox. This led me to hypothesize that exposure to the milder cowpox could confer immunity. In 1796, I tested this by inoculating a young boy, James Phipps, with cowpox matter. When Phipps later showed no ill effects after being exposed to smallpox, my hypothesis was validated. This marked a significant breakthrough in our fight against smallpox.
translatedText: Niliona kwamba wahudumu wa maziwa waliopatwa na ndui walionekana kuwa na kinga dhidi ya ndui. Hii ilinipelekea kudhania kuwa kufichuliwa na ndui ya ng'ombe kunaweza kutoa kinga. Mnamo 1796, nilijaribu hili kwa kumchanja mvulana mdogo, James Phipps, na ugonjwa wa cowpox. Wakati Phipps baadaye hakuonyesha madhara yoyote baada ya kuathiriwa na ndui, dhana yangu ilithibitishwa. Hii iliashiria mafanikio makubwa katika vita vyetu dhidi ya ndui.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Emily Dickinson
translatedText: Emily Dickinson

sourceText: Dickinson experienced profound loneliness, grief from the loss of loved ones, and the societal constraints placed upon women in her era. She increasingly withdrew from society, particularly from the late 1850s onward, becoming a recluse.
translatedText: Dickinson alipata upweke mkubwa, huzuni kutokana na kupoteza wapendwa wake, na vikwazo vya kijamii vilivyowekwa kwa wanawake katika enzi yake. Alizidi kujiondoa kutoka kwa jamii, haswa kutoka mwishoni mwa miaka ya 1850 na kuendelea, na kuwa mtu aliyetengwa.

sourceText: She transformed these internal struggles into her art. Dickinson used poetry as a powerful vehicle to explore her inner life, the natural world, and the profound mysteries of life and death. Her intense focus on writing allowed her to transmute her solitude into creative energy and timeless verse.
translatedText: Alibadilisha mapambano haya ya ndani kuwa sanaa yake. Dickinson alitumia ushairi kama chombo chenye nguvu cha kuchunguza maisha yake ya ndani, ulimwengu asilia, na mafumbo makubwa ya maisha na kifo. Kuzingatia sana uandishi kulimruhusu kubadilisha upweke wake kuwa nishati ya ubunifu na aya isiyo na wakati.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: The severe illness that led to his deafness in later life plunged Goya into profound isolation and despair. He also experienced immense psychological suffering witnessing the brutality of war, political upheaval, and the threat of the Inquisition in Spain, observing human cruelty and societal absurdities.
translatedText: Ugonjwa mbaya ambao ulisababisha uziwi wake katika maisha ya baadaye ulimfanya Goya kutengwa na kukata tamaa. Pia alipata mateso makubwa ya kisaikolojia akishuhudia ukatili wa vita, misukosuko ya kisiasa, na tishio la Baraza la Kuhukumu Wazushi nchini Uhispania, akiona ukatili wa kibinadamu na upuuzi wa kijamii.

sourceText: Despite the physical pain of deafness, Goya delved deeper into his inner world, transforming his experiences into art. Through experimental and intense works like the 'Black Paintings' series, he expressed his suffering and the tragedies of his time, forging an artistic breakthrough. He maintained his resolve as an artist, never abandoning his brush even during his exile.
translatedText: Licha ya maumivu ya kimwili ya uziwi, Goya alizama zaidi katika ulimwengu wake wa ndani, akibadilisha uzoefu wake kuwa sanaa. Kupitia kazi za majaribio na kali kama vile mfululizo wa 'Michoro Nyeusi', alionyesha mateso yake na mikasa ya wakati wake, na kutengeneza mafanikio ya kisanii. Alidumisha azimio lake kama msanii, kamwe hakuacha brashi yake hata wakati wa uhamisho wake.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: George Eliot
translatedText: George Eliot

sourceText: Mary Ann Evans
translatedText: Mary Ann Evans

sourceText: She faced significant social ostracism and criticism in Victorian society for her unconventional relationship with George Henry Lewes, a married man. This, combined with the societal constraints placed upon intellectual women of her time, led to profound personal struggles.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na ubaguzi mkubwa wa kijamii na ukosoaji katika jamii ya Victoria kwa uhusiano wake usio wa kawaida na George Henry Lewes, mwanamume aliyeolewa. Hii, pamoja na vikwazo vya kijamii vilivyowekwa kwa wanawake wasomi wa wakati wake, ilisababisha mapambano makubwa ya kibinafsi.

sourceText: Eliot channeled her intellect and passion into her writing, producing some of the most insightful novels of the era. Her pseudonym allowed her work to be judged independently of her gender. Her enduring partnership with Lewes provided intellectual companionship and emotional support, enabling her to overcome societal disapproval and focus on her literary achievements.
translatedText: Eliot alielekeza akili na mapenzi yake katika uandishi wake, akitoa riwaya zenye utambuzi zaidi za enzi hiyo. Jina lake bandia liliruhusu kazi yake kuhukumiwa bila ya jinsia yake. Ushirikiano wake wa kudumu na Lewes ulitoa ushirikiano wa kiakili na usaidizi wa kihisia, ukimwezesha kushinda kutokubalika kwa jamii na kuzingatia mafanikio yake ya kifasihi.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Henry Hudson
translatedText: Henry Hudson

sourceText: Despite his conviction in his expeditions, he faced mutiny from his crew and the daunting fear of the unknown seas. Ultimately, he failed to find the Northwest Passage and met a tragic end.
translatedText: Licha ya imani yake katika safari zake, alikabiliwa na maasi kutoka kwa wafanyakazi wake na hofu ya kutisha ya bahari isiyojulikana. Hatimaye, alishindwa kupata Njia ya Kaskazini-Magharibi na akakutana na mwisho mbaya.

sourceText: Even amidst setbacks and betrayal, he never lost his spirit of relentless challenge towards the unknown world. Although his journey did not end in personal success, it contributed to redrawing the map of North America.
translatedText: Hata katikati ya vikwazo na usaliti, hakuwahi kupoteza roho yake ya changamoto isiyokoma kuelekea ulimwengu usiojulikana. Ingawa safari yake haikuishia katika mafanikio ya kibinafsi, ilichangia kuchora upya ramani ya Amerika Kaskazini.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Much of Homer's life is shrouded in mystery, and his very existence is debated. Legends of blindness suggest a profound personal struggle.
translatedText: Sehemu kubwa ya maisha ya Homer imegubikwa na fumbo, na kuwepo kwake kunabishaniwa. Hadithi za upofu zinapendekeza mapambano ya kina ya kibinafsi.

sourceText: Despite potential physical impairment, he possessed extraordinary inner vision, drawing inspiration to craft timeless epics that have resonated through millennia.
translatedText: Licha ya ulemavu uliokuwa ukiwezekana, alikuwa na uwezo wa kuona wa ajabu, na hivyo kumtia moyo kuunda tamthilia zisizo na wakati ambazo zimevuma kwa milenia.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Jane Austen
translatedText: Jane Austen

sourceText: It took a considerable time to gain recognition for her talent as a writer, and she faced many restrictions due to her gender when publishing her works. She also experienced financial difficulties and family health issues.
translatedText: Ilichukua muda mrefu kupata kutambuliwa kwa talanta yake kama mwandishi, na alikabiliwa na vikwazo vingi kutokana na jinsia yake wakati wa kuchapisha kazi zake. Pia alipata matatizo ya kifedha na masuala ya afya ya familia.

sourceText: She persevered by consistently writing and receiving support from her family and friends. Her unique literary world was built upon keen observation of society and a profound understanding of human nature.
translatedText: Alivumilia kwa kuandika mara kwa mara na kupokea usaidizi kutoka kwa familia yake na marafiki. Ulimwengu wake wa kipekee wa fasihi ulijengwa juu ya uchunguzi mkali wa jamii na uelewa wa kina wa asili ya mwanadamu.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: John Stuart Mill
translatedText: John Stuart Mill

sourceText: Mill endured a rigorous, intellectually demanding upbringing by his father, James Mill, which led to significant emotional and psychological strain. He also experienced a severe bout of depression in his early twenties.
translatedText: Mill alistahimili malezi magumu na yenye kuhitaji kiakili na baba yake, James Mill, ambayo yalisababisha mkazo mkubwa wa kihemko na kisaikolojia. Pia alipata shida kali ya unyogovu katika miaka yake ya mapema ya ishirini.

sourceText: Despite these challenges, he found solace and emotional release in literature and the arts. His profound intellectual and emotional connection with Harriet Taylor was instrumental in helping him rediscover meaning in life and overcome his difficulties.
translatedText: Licha ya changamoto hizi, alipata faraja na kutolewa kihisia katika fasihi na sanaa. Muunganisho wake wa kina wa kiakili na kihemko na Harriet Taylor ulikuwa muhimu katika kumsaidia kugundua tena maana ya maisha na kushinda shida zake.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Olympe De Gouges
translatedText: Olympe De Gouges

sourceText: As a woman, her political voice was suppressed, and her radical ideas led to her trial and execution during the Reign of Terror.
translatedText: Kama mwanamke, sauti yake ya kisiasa ilikandamizwa, na mawazo yake makubwa yalisababisha kesi yake na kuuawa wakati wa Utawala wa Ugaidi.

sourceText: Her courageous writings and actions inspired later feminist movements, and her ahead-of-her-time ideas are remembered to this day.
translatedText: Maandishi na matendo yake ya ujasiri yaliongoza harakati za wanawake za baadaye, na mawazo yake ya mbele ya wakati yanakumbukwa hadi leo.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Living through the turbulent period of the late Roman Empire, he faced academic disputes and misunderstandings from fellow philosophers while structuring his own thought. Furthermore, he encountered difficulties in widely disseminating his philosophical teachings within the empire.
translatedText: Akiishi katika kipindi cha msukosuko cha Milki ya Roma ya marehemu, alikabiliana na mabishano ya kitaaluma na kutoelewana kutoka kwa wanafalsafa wenzake huku akipanga mawazo yake mwenyewe. Zaidi ya hayo, alikumbana na matatizo katika kueneza sana mafundisho yake ya kifalsafa ndani ya milki hiyo.

sourceText: Despite these challenges, he remained steadfast, pursuing union with the 'One' through inner reflection and meditation. His student Porphyry compiled his lecture notes into the 'Enneads,' ensuring his philosophy was fully transmitted to posterity.
translatedText: Licha ya changamoto hizo, alibaki imara, akifuatilia muungano na 'Mmoja' kupitia tafakari ya ndani na kutafakari. Mwanafunzi wake Porphyry alikusanya maelezo yake ya mihadhara katika 'Enneads,' kuhakikisha falsafa yake imepitishwa kikamilifu kwa wazao.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Roald Amundsen
translatedText: Roald Amundsen

sourceText: Initial expedition hardships, financial struggles, and the harsh, isolating environment of the poles were constant torments. The failed attempt to reach the North Pole and the pressure of the race to the South Pole were significant mental trials.
translatedText: Shida za msafara wa awali, mapambano ya kifedha, na mazingira magumu, ya kujitenga ya miti yalikuwa mateso ya kila wakati. Jaribio lililoshindwa la kufikia Ncha ya Kaskazini na shinikizo la mbio hadi Ncha ya Kusini yalikuwa majaribio makubwa ya kiakili.

sourceText: Meticulous planning, adoption of innovative technologies (airships, airplanes), and above all, an indomitable will allowed him to overcome difficulties. He viewed failures as learning opportunities rather than setbacks, relentlessly pursuing his goals.
translatedText: Kupanga kwa uangalifu, kupitishwa kwa teknolojia za ubunifu (ndege, ndege), na juu ya yote, indomitable itamruhusu kushinda shida. Aliona kushindwa kuwa fursa za kujifunza badala ya vikwazo, akifuatilia miradi yake bila kuchoka.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Elizabeth Cady Stanton
translatedText: Elizabeth Cady Stanton

sourceText: Women were legally considered property of their husbands, denied rights to property, child custody, and even their own earnings. Their voices were socially silenced, and educational opportunities were severely limited.
translatedText: Wanawake walizingatiwa kisheria kuwa mali ya waume zao, walinyimwa haki za mali, malezi ya watoto, na hata mapato yao wenyewe. Sauti zao zilinyamazishwa kijamii, na fursa za elimu zilikuwa chache sana.

sourceText: I was outraged by this injustice and recognized women as intellectual and moral equals to men. Alongside my peers, I drafted the 'Declaration of Sentiments,' ceaselessly speaking and writing to change public perception and demand our inherent rights.
translatedText: Nilikasirishwa na dhulma hii na nikatambua wanawake kuwa sawa na wanaume kiakili na kiadili. Pamoja na vijana wenzangu, nilitayarisha 'Tamko la Hisia,' bila kukoma kusema na kuandika ili kubadilisha mtazamo wa umma na kudai haki zetu za asili.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: Henrik Ibsen
translatedText: Henrik Ibsen

sourceText: He suffered immense financial hardship due to his father's business failures in his youth, and later faced debt collection and societal prejudice as a young man. These struggles deeply influenced his work, infusing it with dark and realistic themes.
translatedText: Alipata shida kubwa ya kifedha kutokana na kushindwa kwa biashara ya baba yake katika ujana wake, na baadaye alikabiliwa na ukusanyaji wa madeni na ubaguzi wa kijamii kama kijana. Mapambano haya yaliathiri sana kazi yake, na kuiingiza katika mada za giza na za kweli.

sourceText: Despite these hardships, Ibsen honed his craft through writing. He keenly observed societal absurdities and exposed them through the medium of theatre, exploring both social and personal truths. His plays gradually gained recognition, bringing him financial stability and renown.
translatedText: Licha ya ugumu huu, Ibsen aliboresha ufundi wake kupitia uandishi. Alichunguza kwa makini mambo ya kipuuzi ya jamii na kuyafichua kupitia njia ya maigizo, akichunguza ukweli wa kijamii na wa kibinafsi. Tamthilia zake taratibu zilipata kutambuliwa, zikimletea utulivu wa kifedha na umaarufu.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: He suffered from chronic health issues, particularly digestive and respiratory ailments. He also grappled with the potential political exploitation of his literary works during a time of great civil unrest.
translatedText: Aliugua magonjwa sugu ya kiafya, haswa magonjwa ya utumbo na kupumua. Pia alipambana na uwezekano wa unyonyaji wa kisiasa wa kazi zake za fasihi wakati wa machafuko makubwa ya wenyewe kwa wenyewe.

sourceText: He found solace and focus in nature and his poetry, channeling his struggles into his art. The support of his friends and patrons, like Maecenas, was crucial in his perseverance to complete his masterpieces.
translatedText: Alipata faraja na umakini katika maumbile na ushairi wake, akielekeza mapambano yake katika sanaa yake. Usaidizi wa marafiki na walinzi wake, kama Maecenas, ulikuwa muhimu katika ustahimilivu wake kukamilisha kazi zake bora.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Majitu ya Historia

sourceText: The breakdown of her marriage to Louis VII, the failures of the Second Crusade, conflicts with her second husband Henry II, and her lengthy imprisonment ordered by Henry II due to her support of her sons' rebellions.
translatedText: Kuvunjika kwa ndoa yake na Louis VII, kushindwa kwa Vita vya Pili vya Msalaba, migogoro na mume wake wa pili Henry II, na kifungo chake cha muda mrefu kilichoamriwa na Henry II kutokana na kuunga mkono uasi wa wanawe.

sourceText: Upon her release from imprisonment, she retained significant political influence, acting as regent for her son Richard I in England and tirelessly working to secure his ransom, demonstrating an unyielding spirit.
translatedText: Alipoachiliwa kutoka gerezani, aliendelea kuwa na uvutano mkubwa wa kisiasa, akiwa kama mwakilishi wa mwanawe Richard wa Kwanza nchini Uingereza na kufanya kazi bila kuchoka ili kupata fidia yake, akionyesha roho ya kutobadilika.

sourceText: Middle Ages
translatedText: Zama za Kati

sourceText: Sundiata Keita
translatedText: Sundiata Keita

sourceText: As a child, I was crippled and an outcast, my right to the throne challenged by my stepmother and her sons. I was forced into exile, facing constant threats and the despair of a lost homeland.
translatedText: Nilipokuwa mtoto, nilikuwa mlemavu na mtu aliyetengwa, haki yangu ya kiti cha enzi ilipingwa na mama yangu wa kambo na wanawe. Nililazimishwa uhamishoni, nikikabili vitisho vya mara kwa mara na kukata tamaa kwa nchi iliyopotea.

sourceText: Yet, the spirit of the lion could not be caged. During my exile, I forged alliances and gathered loyal warriors. I returned with a formidable army, reclaimed my birthright, and united the Mandinka people, transforming them into a formidable empire.
translatedText: Hata hivyo, roho ya simba haikuweza kufungwa. Wakati wa uhamisho wangu, nilitengeneza mashirikiano na kukusanya wapiganaji waaminifu. Nilirudi na jeshi lenye kutisha, nikachukua tena haki yangu ya kuzaliwa, na kuwaunganisha Wamandinka, na kuwageuza kuwa milki yenye kutisha.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 13

sourceText: Maria Theresa
translatedText: Maria Theresa

sourceText: She faced prejudice and challenges as a female ruler in a male-dominated society and endured severe national security and economic difficulties due to intense territorial disputes with Prussia (War of the Austrian Succession, Seven Years' War).
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na ubaguzi na changamoto kama mtawala mwanamke katika jamii iliyotawaliwa na wanaume na alivumilia matatizo makubwa ya usalama wa kitaifa na kiuchumi kutokana na migogoro mikali ya eneo na Prussia (Vita vya Mafanikio ya Austria, Vita vya Miaka Saba).

sourceText: She overcame crises through strong foreign policy and military reforms, and strengthened the empire's centralization by reforming education and judicial systems, improving the lives of her subjects.
translatedText: Alishinda mizozo kupitia sera dhabiti za kigeni na mageuzi ya kijeshi, na akaimarisha uwekaji kati wa ufalme kwa kurekebisha mifumo ya elimu na mahakama, kuboresha maisha ya raia wake.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: After her husband's death, the Romans seized the Iceni lands, flogged Boudicca, and violated her daughters. This profound injustice ignited her fury and resolve for vengeance.
translatedText: Baada ya kifo cha mumewe, Warumi walichukua ardhi ya Iceni, wakampiga Boudicca, na kuwadhulumu binti zake. Udhalimu huu mkubwa ulichochea hasira yake na azimio la kulipiza kisasi.

sourceText: Fueled by this immense suffering and humiliation, Boudicca rallied various British tribes, launching a fierce rebellion. Though ultimately defeated, her resistance struck terror into the Roman invaders and inspired future generations.
translatedText: Akichochewa na mateso haya makubwa na fedheha, Boudicca alikusanya makabila mbalimbali ya Uingereza, na kuanzisha uasi mkali. Ingawa hatimaye alishindwa, upinzani wake ulitia hofu kwa wavamizi wa Kirumi na kuhamasisha vizazi vijavyo.

sourceText: 1st Century AD
translatedText: Karne ya 1 BK

sourceText: She began as a prostitute and was initially subservient to her husband, Zheng Yi. Her fleet also faced constant threats from the Qing government and Portuguese and British naval forces.
translatedText: Alianza kama kahaba na hapo awali alikuwa akimtii mumewe, Zheng Yi. Meli zake pia zilikabiliwa na vitisho vya mara kwa mara kutoka kwa serikali ya Qing na vikosi vya wanamaji vya Ureno na Uingereza.

sourceText: After Zheng Yi's death, she seized control of his fleet and consolidated her power. Through strict codes and brilliant strategy, she suppressed internal dissent and repelled external threats, expanding her influence.
translatedText: Baada ya kifo cha Zheng Yi, alinyakua udhibiti wa meli yake na kuimarisha nguvu zake. Kupitia kanuni kali na mkakati mzuri, alikandamiza upinzani wa ndani na kuzima vitisho vya nje, na kupanua ushawishi wake.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: He faced challenges in consolidating power after overthrowing the previous ruler, Sonni Ali, and dealt with internal dissent and the constant threat of rebellion across a vast territory.
translatedText: Alikumbana na changamoto katika kuimarisha mamlaka baada ya kumpindua mtawala aliyetangulia, Sonni Ali, na kukabiliana na upinzani wa ndani na tishio la mara kwa mara la uasi katika eneo kubwa.

sourceText: Askia the Great implemented a strong centralized administration, standardized weights and measures, established a sophisticated bureaucracy, and promoted Islam as a unifying force. His pilgrimage to Mecca in 1496 also enhanced his prestige and solidified his legitimacy.
translatedText: Askia Mkuu alitekeleza utawala madhubuti wa serikali kuu, mizani na vipimo vilivyosanifiwa, akaanzisha urasimu wa hali ya juu, na akakuza Uislamu kama nguvu inayounganisha. Hija yake ya Mecca mwaka 1496 pia iliimarisha heshima yake na kuimarisha uhalali wake.

sourceText: Late 15th - Early 16th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 15 - Mapema karne ya 16

sourceText: Frequent political upheavals and wars created an unstable research environment, forcing him to move between various regions.
translatedText: Misukosuko ya mara kwa mara ya kisiasa na vita vilitengeneza mazingira ya utafiti yasiyokuwa na msimamo, na kumlazimisha kuhama kati ya mikoa mbalimbali.

sourceText: He never lost his passion for learning, regardless of the adversity. He overcame challenges through constant observation, meticulous documentation, and a dedicated effort to synthesize knowledge from different cultures.
translatedText: Hakuwahi kupoteza shauku yake ya kujifunza, bila kujali shida. Alishinda changamoto kupitia uchunguzi wa mara kwa mara, uwekaji kumbukumbu kwa uangalifu, na bidii ya kujitolea ya kuunganisha maarifa kutoka kwa tamaduni tofauti.

sourceText: 10th-11th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 10-11

sourceText: William Harvey
translatedText: William Harvey

sourceText: The entrenched dogma of Galen, the skepticism from the established scientific community towards novel ideas, and the arduous task of gathering irrefutable evidence for my theories presented significant challenges.
translatedText: Fundisho la imani lililokita mizizi la Galen, mashaka kutoka kwa jumuiya ya kisayansi iliyoanzishwa kuelekea mawazo mapya, na kazi ngumu ya kukusanya ushahidi usiopingika wa nadharia zangu ilileta changamoto kubwa.

sourceText: Through meticulous dissection and experimentation, I presented undeniable proof that blood circulates from the heart, through the body, and back again. My persistent dedication and scientific inquiry eventually earned acceptance within the medical world.
translatedText: Kupitia mgawanyiko wa kina na majaribio, nilitoa uthibitisho usiopingika kwamba damu huzunguka kutoka kwa moyo, kupitia mwili, na kurudi tena. Kujitolea kwangu na uchunguzi wa kisayansi hatimaye ulipata kukubalika katika ulimwengu wa matibabu.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17

sourceText: Joseph Priestley
translatedText: Joseph Priestley

sourceText: Priestley faced significant social and political opposition due to his radical religious and political views. His support for the French Revolution and his dissenting stance against the Church of England led to the destruction of his church and property.
translatedText: Priestley alikabili upinzani mkubwa wa kijamii na kisiasa kutokana na misimamo yake mikali ya kidini na kisiasa. Kuunga mkono Mapinduzi ya Ufaransa na msimamo wake wa kupinga Kanisa la Anglikana kuliongoza kwenye uharibifu wa kanisa na mali yake.

sourceText: Despite persecution, Priestley persevered in his scientific research. He emigrated to the United States, continuing his work in a new environment, and his scientific contributions were eventually recognized and celebrated.
translatedText: Licha ya mateso, Priestley aliendelea kufanya utafiti wake wa kisayansi. Alihamia Merika, akiendelea na kazi yake katika mazingira mapya, na michango yake ya kisayansi hatimaye ilitambuliwa na kusherehekewa.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: Carl Linnaeus
translatedText: Carl Linnaeus

sourceText: Linnaeus faced significant financial hardship and academic challenges early in his career. His groundbreaking ideas on classifying life were also met with skepticism and resistance from established scientific circles.
translatedText: Linnaeus alikabiliwa na matatizo makubwa ya kifedha na changamoto za kitaaluma mapema katika kazi yake. Mawazo yake ya msingi juu ya kuainisha maisha pia yalikutana na mashaka na upinzani kutoka kwa duru za kisayansi zilizoanzishwa.

sourceText: Through relentless observation, extensive fieldwork across Europe, and meticulous documentation, Linnaeus refined his system. His persistence and the undeniable utility of his binomial nomenclature eventually led to its widespread adoption and cemented his legacy.
translatedText: Kupitia uchunguzi usio na huruma, kazi nyingi za uwandani kote Ulaya, na uwekaji kumbukumbu kwa uangalifu, Linnaeus aliboresha mfumo wake. Uvumilivu wake na matumizi yasiyopingika ya nomenclature yake ya binomial hatimaye ilisababisha kupitishwa kwake kuenea na kuimarisha urithi wake.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: Lise Meitner
translatedText: Lise Meitner

sourceText: She faced academic limitations due to her gender and persecution from the Nazi regime because she was Jewish, forcing her into exile. She also experienced the pain of not being recognized for her contribution to the discovery of nuclear fission and not receiving the Nobel Prize.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na mapungufu ya kitaaluma kutokana na jinsia yake na mateso kutoka kwa utawala wa Nazi kwa sababu alikuwa Myahudi, na kumlazimisha uhamishoni. Pia alipata uchungu wa kutotambuliwa kwa mchango wake katika ugunduzi wa mpasuko wa nyuklia na kutopokea Tuzo ya Nobel.

sourceText: Despite adverse circumstances, she never lost her passion for science. She continued her research even in exile and, through collaboration with fellow scientists, completed the theoretical explanation of nuclear fission. Although she did not receive the Nobel Prize, she left a significant mark on the scientific community.
translatedText: Licha ya hali mbaya, hakuwahi kupoteza shauku yake ya sayansi. Aliendelea na utafiti wake hata akiwa uhamishoni na, kupitia ushirikiano na wanasayansi wenzake, alikamilisha maelezo ya kinadharia ya mgawanyiko wa nyuklia. Ingawa hakupokea Tuzo la Nobel, aliacha alama muhimu kwa jamii ya kisayansi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - katikati ya karne ya 20

sourceText: Emmy Noether
translatedText: Emmy Noether

sourceText: Persecution under the Nazi regime and the discrimination and exclusion she faced in academia due to her Jewish heritage.
translatedText: Mateso chini ya utawala wa Nazi na ubaguzi na kutengwa alikabiliana navyo katika taaluma kutokana na urithi wake wa Kiyahudi.

sourceText: After emigrating to the United States, she secured a professorship at Bryn Mawr College, continuing her research and inspiring many young mathematicians.
translatedText: Baada ya kuhamia Marekani, alipata uprofesa katika Chuo cha Bryn Mawr, akiendelea na utafiti wake na kuwatia moyo wanahisabati wengi wachanga.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Alexander von Humboldt
translatedText: Alexander von Humboldt

sourceText: He experienced the early loss of his mother and occasional friction with his brother Wilhelm. His expeditions were often fraught with danger and physically demanding conditions.
translatedText: Alipata hasara ya mapema ya mama yake na msuguano wa mara kwa mara na kaka yake Wilhelm. Safari zake mara nyingi zilijaa hatari na hali ngumu za kimwili.

sourceText: Humboldt's insatiable curiosity and relentless drive for discovery propelled him forward. Through meticulous research, detailed documentation, and collaboration with fellow scientists, he overcame obstacles and solidified his scientific achievements.
translatedText: Udadisi usiotosheka wa Humboldt na msukumo usiokoma wa ugunduzi ulimsogeza mbele. Kupitia utafiti wa kina, nyaraka za kina, na ushirikiano na wanasayansi wenzake, alishinda vikwazo na kuimarisha mafanikio yake ya kisayansi.

sourceText: Late 18th - Mid 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 18 - katikati ya karne ya 19

sourceText: Tycho Brahe
translatedText: Tycho Brahe

sourceText: The conflict between his duties as a nobleman in military and political affairs versus his passion for scholarship. The lifelong complex stemming from the loss of his nose in a duel, and the rivalries and envy from fellow scientists.
translatedText: Mgogoro kati ya majukumu yake kama mtu mashuhuri katika masuala ya kijeshi na kisiasa dhidi ya shauku yake ya usomi. Ugumu wa maisha yote unaotokana na kupoteza pua yake katika duwa, na mashindano na wivu kutoka kwa wanasayansi wenzake.

sourceText: He channeled his talents and passion into astronomical research, achieving significant academic accomplishments and gaining public acclaim. He also attempted to overcome his appearance complex by crafting a metal nose and using masks.
translatedText: Alielekeza talanta na shauku yake katika utafiti wa unajimu, kupata mafanikio makubwa ya kitaaluma na kupata sifa ya umma. Pia alijaribu kushinda mwonekano wake tata kwa kutengeneza pua ya chuma na kutumia vinyago.

sourceText: 16th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 16

sourceText: Muir endured a strict, labor-intensive upbringing in Scotland, suffering frequent injuries and illnesses. A severe factory accident in 1867 nearly cost him his eyesight, a profound physical and emotional trial.
translatedText: Muir alistahimili malezi magumu, yenye nguvu sana huko Scotland, akiugua majeraha ya mara kwa mara na magonjwa. Ajali mbaya ya kiwanda mnamo 1867 ilikaribia kumgharimu macho yake, jaribio kubwa la mwili na kihemko.

sourceText: Confined to darkness after his accident, Muir's recovery was deeply intertwined with his rediscovery of the natural world through memory and imagination. This near-blindness paradoxically sharpened his spiritual connection to nature, fueling his lifelong advocacy for its preservation.
translatedText: Akiwa amefungwa gizani baada ya ajali yake, ahueni ya Muir ilifungamana sana na ugunduzi wake upya wa ulimwengu wa asili kupitia kumbukumbu na mawazo. Upofu huu wa karibu uliimarisha uhusiano wake wa kiroho na asili, na kuchochea utetezi wake wa maisha yote kwa uhifadhi wake.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Alfred Russel Wallace
translatedText: Alfred Russel Wallace

sourceText: Financial struggles, bouts of serious illness, and the challenge of pursuing scientific inquiry often without immediate recognition or support.
translatedText: Mapambano ya kifedha, magonjwa mazito, na changamoto ya kutafuta uchunguzi wa kisayansi mara nyingi bila kutambuliwa au kuungwa mkono mara moja.

sourceText: His profound passion for nature, resilience in the face of adversity, and unwavering dedication to scientific observation allowed him to persevere and make monumental contributions to our understanding of life.
translatedText: Shauku yake ya kina kwa asili, uthabiti katika uso wa dhiki, na kujitolea bila kuyumbayumba kwa uchunguzi wa kisayansi kulimruhusu kustahimili na kutoa mchango mkubwa kwa uelewa wetu wa maisha.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Pierre Curie
translatedText: Pierre Curie

sourceText: He faced financial struggles, lacked adequate laboratory equipment, and suffered from health issues related to his research.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na matatizo ya kifedha, alikosa vifaa vya kutosha vya maabara, na aliteseka kutokana na masuala ya afya kuhusiana na utafiti wake.

sourceText: Through persistent effort and collaboration with his wife, Marie Curie, he overcame these challenges and achieved significant scientific breakthroughs.
translatedText: Kupitia juhudi na ushirikiano wa kudumu na mke wake, Marie Curie, alishinda changamoto hizi na kupata mafanikio makubwa ya kisayansi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Charles Lyell
translatedText: Charles Lyell

sourceText: Lyell faced significant opposition from the scientific establishment for his radical theory of uniformitarianism, which challenged prevailing views of Earth's history. Gathering sufficient evidence to support his ideas, often in remote and challenging locations, also presented considerable hardship and academic isolation.
translatedText: Lyell alikabiliwa na upinzani mkubwa kutoka kwa taasisi ya kisayansi kwa nadharia yake kali ya umoja, ambayo ilipinga maoni yaliyopo ya historia ya Dunia. Kukusanya ushahidi wa kutosha ili kuunga mkono mawazo yake, mara nyingi katika maeneo ya mbali na yenye changamoto, pia kulileta ugumu mkubwa na kutengwa kwa masomo.

sourceText: Through meticulous fieldwork and the systematic accumulation of evidence, Lyell steadfastly defended his principles. 'Principles of Geology' became the definitive text for decades, and his concept of gradual change profoundly influenced not only geology but also evolutionary biology, eventually earning him widespread recognition and respect.
translatedText: Kupitia kazi ya uwandani ya uangalifu na mkusanyiko wa utaratibu wa ushahidi, Lyell alitetea kanuni zake kwa uthabiti. 'Kanuni za Jiolojia' zikawa maandishi mahususi kwa miongo kadhaa, na dhana yake ya mabadiliko ya taratibu iliathiri sana sio tu jiolojia bali pia biolojia ya mageuzi, hatimaye kumfanya atambuliwe na kuheshimiwa kote.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Al-Farabi faced significant challenges due to the religious and political turmoil of his era, often finding his philosophical ideas misunderstood or criticized. He navigated a world where intellectual pursuits could be fraught with peril.
translatedText: Al-Farabi alikabiliwa na changamoto kubwa kutokana na msukosuko wa kidini na kisiasa wa enzi yake, mara nyingi akipata mawazo yake ya kifalsafa kutoeleweka au kukosolewa. Alizunguka ulimwengu ambapo shughuli za kiakili zinaweza kujazwa na hatari.

sourceText: Through relentless scholarship and prolific writing, he meticulously developed his philosophical system. He engaged with leading intellectuals of his time, disseminating his ideas and securing a lasting legacy of profound wisdom that influenced generations.
translatedText: Kupitia usomi usiokoma na uandishi mwingi, aliendeleza kwa uangalifu mfumo wake wa falsafa. Alijishughulisha na wasomi wakuu wa wakati wake, akisambaza mawazo yake na kupata urithi wa kudumu wa hekima ya kina ambayo iliathiri vizazi.

sourceText: 10th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 10

sourceText: Thomas More
translatedText: Thomas More

sourceText: He faced immense pressure from King Henry VIII to accept his divorce and acknowledge him as the Supreme Head of the Church of England. This defiance of his religious convictions led to his imprisonment in the Tower of London and eventual execution.
translatedText: Alikabili shinikizo kubwa kutoka kwa Mfalme Henry wa Nane akubali talaka yake na kumkiri kuwa Mkuu Mkuu wa Kanisa la Uingereza. Kukaidi imani zake za kidini kulimfanya afungwe katika Mnara wa London na hatimaye kuuawa.

sourceText: Despite the threat of torture and death, More remained steadfast in his conscience and beliefs. He chose martyrdom to uphold his principles, leaving a legacy that continues to inspire.
translatedText: Licha ya tisho la kuteswa na kuuawa, More aliendelea kuwa thabiti katika dhamiri na imani yake. Alichagua kifo cha kishahidi ili kushikilia kanuni zake, akiacha urithi unaoendelea kutia moyo.

sourceText: Renaissance
translatedText: Renaissance

sourceText: Simone Weil
translatedText: Simone Weil

sourceText: She endured immense physical and mental suffering. Frail from childhood, her health deteriorated due to hunger and exile during World War II. Her deep empathy for social injustice and human suffering also caused her great anguish.
translatedText: Alivumilia mateso makubwa ya kimwili na kiakili. Akiwa dhaifu tangu utotoni, afya yake ilidhoofika kwa sababu ya njaa na uhamisho wakati wa Vita vya Kidunia vya pili. Huruma yake kubwa kwa ukosefu wa haki wa kijamii na kuteseka kwa wanadamu pia ilimletea huzuni kubwa.

sourceText: Rather than avoiding suffering, she confronted and sought to understand it. By working alongside laborers and witnessing the horrors of war firsthand, she experienced the extremes of the human condition. Through these experiences, she further realized the importance of solidarity and love, transforming them into her philosophy and actions.
translatedText: Badala ya kuepuka kuteseka, alikabiliana nayo na kutafuta kuelewa. Kwa kufanya kazi pamoja na vibarua na kujionea maovu ya vita, alipitia hali mbaya ya kibinadamu. Kupitia uzoefu huu, alitambua zaidi umuhimu wa mshikamano na upendo, na kuyabadilisha kuwa falsafa na matendo yake.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 20

sourceText: Antonio Gramsci
translatedText: Antonio Gramsci

sourceText: Imprisoned for over a decade under the Fascist regime, he endured severe physical and mental suffering. His health deteriorated drastically, and communication with the outside world was heavily restricted.
translatedText: Akiwa gerezani kwa zaidi ya miaka kumi chini ya utawala wa Kifashisti, alivumilia mateso makali ya kimwili na kiakili. Afya yake ilizorota sana, na mawasiliano na ulimwengu wa nje yalikuwa na vizuizi vikali.

sourceText: Despite extreme limitations, he continued to develop his philosophical thought by writing the 'Prison Notebooks'. This led to the consolidation of his intellectual legacy.
translatedText: Licha ya mapungufu makubwa, aliendelea kukuza mawazo yake ya kifalsafa kwa kuandika 'Madaftari ya Magereza'. Hii ilisababisha kuunganishwa kwa urithi wake wa kiakili.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: Rosa Luxemburg
translatedText: Rosa Luxembourg

sourceText: She endured political persecution, imprisonment, and the profound grief of losing comrades during the devastating First World War.
translatedText: Alivumilia mnyanyaso wa kisiasa, kufungwa gerezani, na huzuni kuu ya kupoteza wandugu wakati wa Vita vya Kwanza vya Ulimwengu.

sourceText: Even from within prison walls, she continued to write and articulate her unwavering belief in revolution, never losing hope.
translatedText: Hata akiwa ndani ya kuta za gereza, aliendelea kuandika na kueleza imani yake isiyoyumbayumba ya mapinduzi, bila kukata tamaa.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: George Santayana
translatedText: George Santayana

sourceText: His early life was marked by the absence of his mother and a solitary childhood in Spain, leaving deep emotional scars. He also experienced an identity crisis navigating between two cultures.
translatedText: Maisha yake ya utotoni yaliwekwa alama ya kutokuwepo kwa mama yake na utoto wake peke yake huko Uhispania, na kuacha makovu mazito ya kihemko. Pia alipata shida ya utambulisho kati ya tamaduni mbili.

sourceText: He found solace and overcame his struggles through intense academic pursuit and introspective writing. His professorship at Harvard and prolific literary output provided both a sanctuary and a means to engage with the world.
translatedText: Alipata faraja na akashinda mapambano yake kwa kufuata sana masomo na uandishi wa ndani. Uprofesa wake katika Harvard na matokeo ya fasihi mengi yalitoa patakatifu na njia ya kujihusisha na ulimwengu.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - katikati ya karne ya 20

sourceText: Henri Bergson
translatedText: Henri Bergson

sourceText: As a Jew, he faced antisemitism and the turmoil of World War II, leading to introspection about faith and identity. His philosophy was also criticized as radical by some contemporaries.
translatedText: Akiwa Myahudi, alikabiliana na chuki dhidi ya Wayahudi na misukosuko ya Vita vya Pili vya Ulimwengu, na hivyo kumfanya afikiriwe kuhusu imani na utambulisho wake. Falsafa yake pia ilikosolewa kama kali na watu wengine wa wakati huo.

sourceText: Bergson confronted these challenges with the depth and rigor of his philosophical system. He affirmed his Jewish identity while fulfilling his duties as a Frenchman, persistently developing his ideas, which eventually led to academic recognition, including the Nobel Prize in Literature.
translatedText: Bergson alikabiliana na changamoto hizi kwa kina na ukali wa mfumo wake wa falsafa. Alithibitisha utambulisho wake wa Kiyahudi wakati akitimiza wajibu wake kama Mfaransa, akiendelea kuendeleza mawazo yake, ambayo hatimaye ilisababisha kutambuliwa kitaaluma, ikiwa ni pamoja na Tuzo ya Nobel ya Fasihi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: He faced significant persecution, including being summoned for heresy by the Pope and imprisonment. His criticisms of the Church's authority and complex theological doctrines led to accusations of deviation from orthodoxy.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na mateso makubwa, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuitwa kwa ajili ya uzushi na Papa na kufungwa gerezani. Ukosoaji wake wa mamlaka ya Kanisa na mafundisho changamano ya kitheolojia ulisababisha shutuma za kukengeuka kutoka kwa mafundisho ya kweli.

sourceText: Ockham countered these challenges with his rigorous logic and clarity of thought. By providing concise and clear explanations for complex theological and philosophical problems, he defended his ideas and paved the way for future intellectual movements.
translatedText: Ockham alikabiliana na changamoto hizi kwa mantiki yake kali na uwazi wa mawazo. Kwa kutoa maelezo mafupi na yaliyo wazi kwa ajili ya matatizo changamano ya kitheolojia na kifalsafa, alitetea mawazo yake na kuandaa njia kwa ajili ya harakati za kiakili za wakati ujao.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 14
